title: Are Seele the FAR
wikitext:
[]: Are Seele the FAR?             Quote Post by qu4d » Thu Aug 21, 2014 7:25 am [Renamed thread from "Kubrick, Clarke and some very old people" to "Are Seele the FAR?" - OMF] Introduction In which year was Shinji born? 2001. Which is the name of a film by Stanley Kubrick, that he had written with Arthur C. Clarke. It's nothing new that NGE was influenced by that story... but I couldn't really find a thread discussing that in more detail. So here it is. In the movie 2001, there are those monoliths, that trigger the use of tools/weapons and thus evolution/civilization. But Kubrick decided to not explain their origin and purpose - it is only hinted/implicated that they are not from earth. Their origin Arthur C. Clarke on the other hand went into detail and even wrote 3 sequels: 2010, 2061 and 3001. In his novel 2001, there is one chapter giving those fascinating blocks some background. 2001 - Chapter 37 - Experiment Full chapter in the spoiler: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Aug 21, 2014 7:36 am We kind of stumbled upon this theory before when discussing the specifics of what the Seele “Program” was doing with Mark.09. It’s nice to see a thread dedicated to further exploring this idea, though, as well as pointing out the creative reference points that Anno may have been drawing from for inspiration. I haven’t read 3001 yet, so this was a welcomed revelation for me. (I don’t consider it a spoiler violation simply because I’m 26 years old, and probably should have just read the book already.) I have my hesitations of accusing Seele of being FAR simply because how loaded of a statement it is for this continuity. But either way, it still seems a conclusion one could arrive at with the evidence given thus far, even though I feel that it may need further context in order to substantiate itself.

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Thu Aug 21, 2014 9:15 am SEELE being FAR is wow I didnt saw it like that The movie take a new understanding then But if it's them , why the purpose of the seed of life then ?

[]:             Quote Post by thezeppo1138 » Thu Aug 21, 2014 9:24 am Yeah I thought the same thing when I saw that part in 3.0. I was thinking, as well, they might be an ancient race, if not the ancestral race. I really love that convo with them in 3.0, I instantly thought, "Holy crap! They're just like the monoliths in 2001 in this version!" 3.0 seems the most like 2001 yet: The tone of the film, use of color, our first classical piece in Rebuild, the NERV food tray dispenser, Shinji's space suit, Seele being the monoliths and possibly helping forward our civilization as well as trying to advance our evolution through Instrumentality, part of the movie tales place in space, the time skip, and whatever else I can't think of or haven't noticed yet. It's one of the reasons I love 3.0 so much.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Aug 21, 2014 9:54 am I do not think Seele's goal was ever quite so benign. I'd be surprised if Gendo's goal was much better. It'd be neat if some of this was true, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Aug 21, 2014 1:24 pm NGE Shinji may have been born in '01 -- but the chronology in NTE is not so clear, given the release date.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Aug 21, 2014 3:06 pm ^ Especially since there are hints that 1.0 and 2.0 happened in the years 2017 and 2018.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Aug 21, 2014 4:01 pm Shinji can be born before Second Impact and still have no memory of what it was like. Fourteen to sixteen is a big difference. Sixteen year olds are much different from fourteen year olds (thirteen in Asuka's case). I can't see them aging them up like that, but only mentioning it in minor background details that have no bearing on anything else.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Thu Aug 21, 2014 4:05 pm Are you guys now really talking about the date? It was just a (not so creative and kinda lame) attempt to mention 2001. Topic: SEELE = FAR

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Thu Aug 21, 2014 4:21 pm Gendo wrote: This is farewell. Even though all of you have changed the shape of your souls, you are still lifeforms blessed with the Fruit of Knowledge . You were able to live for eternity, but, same as us, you still cannot escape the fate of death. To advance the evolution of the flock burdened with death, you had bestowed upon us the gift of civilization. As the representative of humanity, I say thank you. Through death, I shall return your souls to where they belong. The long desired Human Instrumentality Project, and the contracted deicide, will be completed by me.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Thu Aug 21, 2014 4:27 pm Sorrow wrote: There is nothing to suggest that they weren't once human.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Thu Aug 21, 2014 4:33 pm Representative: a person chosen or appointed (even if self elected) to speak on behalf of others. Gendo assumed this role as he is in a very high position amongst humanity and thanks SEELE--the pioneers or leaders of humanity up until this point--on humanity's behalf.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Thu Aug 21, 2014 5:06 pm But why would he even address this, if SEELE are human?! I don't see how this would make sense. Gendo speaks on behalf of humanity, sure. But speaking on behalf of a group implies speaking to another group. Can SEELE be part of both groups?

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Thu Aug 21, 2014 5:15 pm Well, not necessarily. A spokesman may address his own group and speak to them, on behalf of them. It's enough that SEELE are in a different position, they need not be inhuman, or have never been human, also. To me, Gendo is simply thanking the leaders of humanity on behalf of humanity. It seems as though SEELE have led people all this way since civilisation began, for their own agenda. Though a person has now come along and betrayed them (Gendo - who is changing their plans somewhat) he is still thankful to them and sees that although the rest of humanity may not know who they are or what they have given them, humanity ought to, and would be, thankful to them. For this he also tries to assure them they should be happy with the results even if it isn't exactly as they planned. It seems that simple, to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Zoop » Fri Aug 22, 2014 8:21 am Very interesting stuff, makes me want to read the sequels to 2001. Seele supposedly gave mankind it's civilisation. Which would mean mankind was without it before Seele came along. How literal should we take the word civilisation? Does it mean intelligence? Or did they simply help build roads and houses or something. I think this bit right here is a homage to 2001. The monolith that actually triggers some kind of evolution to advance a species to a more intelligent one. If this is the case, then Seele can't possible be human right? I mean, they helped evolve a lower species into an intelligent one, there were no homo erectus to be found on earth at that point. So if they are human, then at least not humans of earth ... Maybe impact mechanics has something to with it. Why do we call 2nd impact, the 2nd? The first one was the meteor that killed the dinosaurs right? What if it was an actual impact, and maybe Seele crawled out of it's vortex.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Fri Aug 22, 2014 1:16 pm Zoop wrote: The monolith that actually triggers some kind of evolution to advance a species to a more intelligent one. If this is the case, then Seele can't possible be human right?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Aug 22, 2014 1:21 pm "We're responsible for civlization" is pretty bog standard mystical order mumbo jumbo.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Fri Aug 22, 2014 1:46 pm Chuckman wrote: "We're responsible for civlization" is pretty bog standard mystical order mumbo jumbo.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Aug 22, 2014 1:56 pm What I wonder is if they were really several thousand year old and helped start human civilization, how the hell did they managed to transfer their souls to monoliths of what appears to be core material in the prehistory? If Gendo's assessment was correct, SEELE were cavemen when they started their organization, so how did they found a way to transfer their soul? Did something or someone else did that to them? Was it Lilith?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Aug 22, 2014 2:20 pm Sorrow wrote: Basically. They're just a very, very old organisation--consisting of humans--that have been leading humanity for more or less as long as they've been an organisation.
